post.description: Configures the global AI provider that will be used by all agents that don't have a specific provider set. This is stored securely as a tenant option. Requires admin privileges.

requestBody.description: Provider configuration including name, model, and authentication details

get.description: Retrieves the currently configured global AI provider. The API key is masked for security. Returns 404 if no global provider is configured. Requires admin privileges.

post.description: Creates a new text-based AI agent with the specified configuration. Text agents generate conversational responses and can use tools to perform tasks. Requires admin privileges.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: If the created Agent should get returned, the accept header must be 'application/json'. Otherwise, only the status code 201 is returned.

post.summary: Create a new AI text agent

get.description: Retrieves a list of all available text agents, including both private agents and shared agents from applications.

200.description: List of text agents retrieved successfully

post.description: Creates a new object-based AI agent that generates structured JSON objects according to a defined schema. Object agents are useful for extracting structured data from prompts. Requires admin privileges.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: If the created Agent should get returned, the accept header must be 'application/json'. Otherwise, only the status code 201 is returned.

agent.system: Extract structured data from the given text.
Extract structured data from the given text.

post.summary: Create a new AI object agent

get.description: Retrieves a list of all available object agents, including both private agents and shared agents from applications.

200.description: List of object agents retrieved successfully

agent.system: Extract structured data from the given text.

put.description: Updates an existing text agent with new configuration. All properties of the agent can be modified including provider settings, tools, and system prompts. Requires admin privileges.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the text agent to update

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: If the updated Agent should get returned, the accept header must be 'application/json'. Otherwise, only the status code 200 is returned.

get.description: Retrieves the complete configuration and details of a specific text agent by name. Can optionally resolve agents from application context using the fromApp parameter.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the text agent

parameters.name: fromApp
parameters.description:
If used, the agent is resolved from the given application context. This is useful, if agents are delivered as bundle
    from an plugin or application, as always the correct version of the agent will be used. The value given, needs to be the context path of
    the application (e.g. 'my-app' for an application available under /apps/my-app or my-app@1.0.1 for a versioned plugin available under /apps/my-app@1.0.1).
    
    Still, if a local agent with the same name exists, this one will have precedence over the application based agent.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a text agent. Only private agents can be deleted - shared agents from applications cannot be removed. Requires admin privileges.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the text agent to delete

delete.summary: Delete a text agent by name

post.description: Sends a message or prompt to a text agent and receives a response. Supports both streaming (SSE/NDJSON) and non-streaming modes. The agent can use configured tools to perform actions and provide contextual responses.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the text agent to talk to

parameters.name: fullResponse
parameters.description:
If set to true, the full response (including details about tool usage etc.) from the AI model is returned. Otherwise, only the text content is returned.
      In a streaming scenario (when the 'Accept' header is set to 'text/event-stream' or 'application/x-ndjson'), the response is a SSE stream.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: In a streaming scenario (when the 'Accept' header is set to 'text/event-stream' or 'application/x-ndjson'), the response is a SSE stream.

parameters.name: fromApp
parameters.description:
If used, the agent is resolved from the given application context. This is useful, if agents are delivered as bundle
    from an plugin or application, as always the correct version of the agent will be used. The value given, needs to be the context path of
    the application (e.g. 'my-app' for an application available under /apps/my-app or my-app@1.0.1 for a versioned plugin available under /apps/my-app@1.0.1).
    
    Still, if a local agent with the same name exists, this one will have precedence over the application based agent.

requestBody.description: Text completion request containing messages or prompt

200.description: Response from text agent. Returns plain text when fullResponse=false, or a detailed object when fullResponse=true.

post.summary: Talk to the named text agent

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the object agent to update

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: If the updated Agent should get returned, the accept header must be 'application/json'. Otherwise, only the status code 200 is returned.

parameters.description: If set to true, the full response (including details about tool usage etc.) from the AI model is returned. Otherwise, only the object content is returned.

parameters.name: fromApp
parameters.description:
If used, the agent is resolved from the given application context. This is useful, if agents are delivered as bundle
    from an plugin or application, as always the correct version of the agent will be used. The value given, needs to be the context path of
    the application (e.g. 'my-app' for an application available under /apps/my-app or my-app@1.0.1 for a versioned plugin available under /apps/my-app@1.0.1).
    
    Still, if a local agent with the same name exists, this one will have precedence over the application based agent.

get.description: Performs a health check on an agent to verify it exists and is properly configured. Returns information about the agent's availability, provider configuration, and any issues that might prevent its use.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the agent to check

parameters.name: fromApp
parameters.description:
If used, the agent is resolved from the given application context. This is useful, if agents are delivered as bundle
    from an plugin or application, as always the correct version of the agent will be used. The value given, needs to be the context path of
    the application (e.g. 'my-app' for an application available under /apps/my-app or my-app@1.0.1 for a versioned plugin available under /apps/my-app@1.0.1).
    
    Still, if a local agent with the same name exists, this one will have precedence over the application based agent.

get.summary: Allows to verify if an agent can be used

post.description: Tests a text agent configuration without persisting it to storage. Useful for validating agent settings and prompts before creating the agent. Supports both streaming and non-streaming modes. Requires admin privileges.

parameters.name: fullResponse
parameters.description:
If set to true, the full response (including details about tool usage etc.) from the AI model is returned. Otherwise, only the text content is returned.
      In a streaming scenario (when the 'Accept' header is set to 'text/event-stream' or 'application/x-ndjson'), the response is a SSE stream.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: In a streaming scenario (when the 'Accept' header is set to 'text/event-stream' or 'application/x-ndjson'), the response is a SSE stream.

200.description: Test response from text agent (streaming or non-streaming)

post.summary: Allows to test an agent without storing it (only for admins)

post.description: Tests an object agent configuration without persisting it to storage. Useful for validating the schema and prompts before creating the agent. Returns a structured object according to the provided schema. Requires admin privileges.

parameters.name: fullResponse
parameters.description: If set to true, the full response (including details about tool usage etc.) from the AI model is returned. Otherwise, only the object content is returned.

info.title: Cumulocity AI Agents
info.description: The Cumulocity AI Agents API description

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Manage and interact with AI agents (text and object types)

tags.name: Provider
tags.description: Configure the global LLM provider settings. You must set a provider before creating agents. Tested providers are Anthropic, Google and OpenAI.

tags.name: Tools
tags.description: Retrieve available tools from configured MCP servers

tags.name: MCP Servers
tags.description: Manage Model Context Protocol (MCP) server configurations

system.description:
System message to include in the prompt.
Can be used with `prompt` or `messages`.

prompt.description:
A simple text prompt or array of messages.
Use either `prompt` or `messages`, but not both.

messages.description:
A list of messages for conversation history.
Use either `prompt` or `messages`, but not both.

stopSequences.description:
Stop sequences. If set, the model will stop generating
text when one of the stop sequences is generated.

variables.description:
Variables to replace in the prompt/messages.
Use {{ variableName }} syntax in prompts.

maxOutputTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate.

temperature.description:
Temperature setting. Controls randomness in the output.
Higher values = more creative, lower values = more deterministic.
Cannot be used together with topP.

topP.description:
Nucleus sampling. Only tokens with top P probability mass are considered.
E.g., 0.1 means only tokens with top 10% probability are considered.
Cannot be used together with temperature.

topK.description:
Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token.
Used to remove "long tail" low probability responses.

presencePenalty.description:
Presence penalty. Affects likelihood of repeating information from the prompt.
Range: -1 (increase repetition) to 1 (decrease repetition).

frequencyPenalty.description:
Frequency penalty. Affects likelihood of repeatedly using same words/phrases.
Range: -1 (increase repetition) to 1 (decrease repetition).

seed.description:
Seed for random sampling. If set and supported by the model,
calls will generate deterministic results.

maxRetries.description:
Maximum number of retries. Set to 0 to disable retries.
Default: 2

timeout.description:
Timeout in milliseconds. The call will be aborted if it takes longer
than the specified timeout. Can be used alongside abortSignal.

schema.description:
JSON Schema that defines the structure of the generated object.
The model will generate an object conforming to this schema.

system.description:
System message to include in the prompt.
Can be used with `prompt` or `messages`.

prompt.description:
A simple text prompt or array of messages.
Use either `prompt` or `messages`, but not both.

messages.description:
A list of messages for conversation history.
Use either `prompt` or `messages`, but not both.

variables.description:
Variables to replace in the prompt/messages.
Use {{ variableName }} syntax in prompts.

maxOutputTokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate.

temperature.description:
Temperature setting. Controls randomness in the output.
Higher values = more creative, lower values = more deterministic.
Cannot be used together with topP.

topP.description:
Nucleus sampling. Only tokens with top P probability mass are considered.
E.g., 0.1 means only tokens with top 10% probability are considered.
Cannot be used together with temperature.

topK.description:
Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token.
Used to remove "long tail" low probability responses.

presencePenalty.description:
Presence penalty. Affects likelihood of repeating information from the prompt.
Range: -1 (increase repetition) to 1 (decrease repetition).

frequencyPenalty.description:
Frequency penalty. Affects likelihood of repeatedly using same words/phrases.
Range: -1 (increase repetition) to 1 (decrease repetition).

seed.description:
Seed for random sampling. If set and supported by the model,
calls will generate deterministic results.

maxRetries.description:
Maximum number of retries. Set to 0 to disable retries.
Default: 2

timeout.description:
Timeout in milliseconds. The call will be aborted if it takes longer
than the specified timeout. Can be used alongside abortSignal.
